content.question: To what extent does the per-capita SNI funding multiplier (2x–3x) create a measurable fiscal incentive for schools to classify marginal/borderline students as SNI, and how much of the documented enrollment increase is attributable to this mechanism versus true prevalence or legitimate diagnostic improvement?
content.reduction_path: Obtain school-level administrative data on SNI classification rates, funding flows, and enrollment trends, and use difference-in-differences or instrumental-variable methods to isolate the independent effect of funding multipliers on classification after controlling for school size, composition, and demographic trends
